fundamentals.name: MongoDB, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MongoDB, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides general purpose database platform worldwide. The company offers MongoDB Atlas, a hosted multi-cloud database-as-a-service solution; MongoDB Enterprise Advanced, a commercial database server for enterprise customers to run in the cloud, on-premises, or in a hybrid environment; and Community Server, a free-to-download version of its database, which includes the functionality that developers need to get started with MongoDB. It offers professional services comprising consulting and training. The company was formerly known as 10gen, Inc. and changed its name to MongoDB, Inc. in August 2013. MongoDB, Inc. was incorporated in 2007 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue and free cash flow, but currently unprofitable on a net basis and carries significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 11.5%; next earnings date is 2026-08-26.
positives: Revenue growth 26.7% YoY — strong top-line expansion.
Free cash flow (TTM) $463.32M — positive cash generation.
negatives: Profit margin -2.9% and Operating margin 0.0% — company is not yet net profitable.
Return on equity -2.5% and Return on assets -2.4% — poor returns on capital.
Forward P/E 45.86x — valuation is demanding given current profitability.

news.paragraph: MongoDB reported Q1 earnings that beat analyst expectations, driven by strong revenue growth and AI-related gains, though the stock saw muted reaction. Several analysts maintained their ratings following the report, with price targets adjusted upwards by Barclays and Wedbush, while Guggenheim and Cantor Fitzgerald kept theirs steady. The company's ability to translate AI enthusiasm into sustained earnings growth will be key as it heads into its next earnings report.

headlines.title: MongoDB Beats Earnings, But Shares Hit Pause Despite AI Gains - MongoDB (NASDAQ:MDB)
MDB Reiterates by Cantor Fitzgerald -- Price Target Maintained a
MDB Maintained by Barclays -- Price Target Raised to $387
MDB Reiterated by Guggenheim -- Price Target Maintained at $475
MDB Maintained by Wedbush -- Price Target Raised to $390
